site.title: Cabaña Sancibrian | Premium Accommodation in Sapzurro, Colombia
site.description: Premium accommodation steps from the beach in Sapzurro, Colombia. Starlink WiFi, 24/7 electricity & Hangar Café coworking — behind Playa Bonita. The Caribbean base for digital nomads & adventurers.

hero.headline: Disconnect to Reconnect Or Don't. We Have Power & Starlink 24/7
hero.subheadline: Your Oasis at the Crossroads of Continents
hero.description: Experience the perfect blend of remote work and tropical paradise in Sapzurro, Colombia's hidden gem.

captainStory.title: A Tribute to Captain Diego Domínguez Escobar
captainStory.subtitle: Where Aviation Legacy Meets Caribbean Paradise
captainStory.story: Cabaña Sancibrian honors the life and legacy of Captain Diego Domínguez Escobar, whose spirit of adventure and exploration lives on in every corner of this retreat steps from the beach. From vintage propellers to classic aviation posters, the cabin celebrates the golden age of flight while offering modern comforts in one of Colombia's most pristine destinations.
captainStory.quote: "The airplane has unveiled for us the true face of the earth. For centuries, roads deceived us." — Antoine de Saint-Exupéry, Wind, Sand and Stars

features.sectionDescription: Where modern amenities meet Caribbean paradise. Everything you need for the perfect remote work retreat.

items.title: 24/7 Electricity
items.description: Air conditioning on grid power. Personal fans on BatX battery backup. One of the few in the region with zero interruptions.

items.title: Hangar Café

items.title: Netflix + Alexa

items.title: San Blas Gateway

testimonials.title: What Our Guests Say
testimonials.description: Real experiences from travelers who've discovered their own paradise at Cabaña Sancibrian.

items.name: Raul
items.text: The place is amazing — there is no accommodation like this in Sapzurro. Very comfortable, with everything you need and excellent service. Highly recommended.

items.name: Montoya
items.text: Very nice, comfortable, quiet, and in an excellent location. The host is attentive to everything and completely professional. 100% recommended.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.description: Everything you need to know before your adventure.

items.question: How do I get to Sapzurro?
items.answer: The fastest route is flying to Acandí airport (1h from Medellín), then a short boat ride to Sapzurro (30-40 min). Alternatively, fly to Apartadó or Montería, ground transport to Necoclí (2-3h), then speedboat to Sapzurro (1.5-2h). We can help coordinate all transportation.

items.question: What's included in my stay?
items.answer: Accommodation with Starlink WiFi, 24/7 electricity, air conditioning (on grid power), personal fans (on battery backup), premium linens and towels, and access to Hangar Café. The outdoor herbal bath (wood-fire heated, with flowers and relaxing herbs) is available upon prior request. Breakfast and other meals also available upon request.

items.question: Is there cell phone signal?
items.answer: Cell signal is limited and depends on your carrier. However, we offer high-speed Starlink WiFi to keep you connected.

items.question: What's the best time to visit?
items.answer: Sapzurro is beautiful year-round. December–April is high season (dry weather + Easter week). July–August is also high season. May–June and September–November are quieter with better prices and lush green scenery.

items.question: Can I work remotely from the cabin?
items.answer: Absolutely! We're designed for digital nomads. Starlink internet, 24/7 power, spacious desk, and Hangar Café coworking space make it perfect for remote work.

items.question: Is Sapzurro safe?
items.answer: Very safe! It's a small fishing village with a friendly community. The main risk is falling in love with the place and never wanting to leave!

sapzurro.title: Sapzurro: The Caribbean's Best-Kept Secret
sapzurro.subtitle: At the Crossroads of Continents
sapzurro.description: Nestled in the heart of the Colombian Darién, Sapzurro is a small fishing village where the jungle meets the sea. Accessible only by boat, this paradise offers a unique experience of total disconnection.
highlights: Capurganá: Neighboring town with more services
Fastest departure point to San Blas Islands (Panama)
sapzurro.strategicLocation: Sancibrian is your fastest gateway to the San Blas Islands (Guna Yala, Panama). Join our all-inclusive 4–5 night expeditions with Akuatours: depart directly from Sapzurro, sleep on 5 pristine private islands, and return to Colombian hospitality. No easier route to paradise exists.

activities.intro: From San Blas island expeditions to jungle hikes and coral reef snorkeling, Sapzurro is your gateway to adventures found nowhere else on Earth.

items.title: San Blas Islands Expedition
items.description: The fastest route to the San Blas Islands (Guna Yala, Panama) starts right here. All-inclusive 4–5 night package with Akuatours: 2 nights in Sapzurro + 2–3 nights on 5 private islands. Immigration, transfers, accommodation, and meals all included. We coordinate everything.

items.title: Snorkeling & Diving at Cabo Tiburón
items.description: Explore vibrant coral reefs at Cabo Tiburón — the first beach on the South American continent. Crystal-clear Caribbean waters with stunning marine life await.

items.title: Hike to La Miel
items.description: 20-minute jungle trail to a pristine Panamanian beach. No passport needed — just bring your ID to register at the border post.

items.title: Kayaking or Paddle Around the Bay
items.description: Paddle along the coastline and discover hidden coves in the turquoise Caribbean waters.

items.title: Bird Watching
items.description: Spot exotic species — toucans, parrots, and endemic birds — in the Darién's rich biodiversity.

items.title: Artisanal Fishing
items.description: Try your luck with local fishermen in the Caribbean waters.

items.title: Trek to Capurganá
items.description: Follow the jungle trail connecting Sapzurro to the charming village of Capurganá (~2 hours on foot). Dense tropical rainforest, coastal views, and pure adventure.

items.title: Local Gastronomy
items.description: Savor fresh Caribbean seafood at Cabaña Cangrejales and local restaurants. From cazuela de mariscos to whole grilled fish — the flavors of the Darién coast.

items.title: Visit Capurganá, Playa Soledad & El Aguacate
items.description: Boat tour from Sapzurro: first stop Capurganá, then Playa Soledad and El Aguacate. Local food included at destination. A full day of Darién beaches with water transport from door to door.

items.title: Simply Relax
items.description: Hammock, book, and the sound of waves. Pure bliss.

activities.startAdventureDesc: We can help arrange activities, tours, and local guides.

howToGetHere.title: Your Journey to Paradise
howToGetHere.subtitle: All roads (and boats) lead to Sapzurro
howToGetHere.intro: Sapzurro is accessible only by boat, making it a true hidden paradise. The journey is part of the adventure! We can help coordinate your transportation from any starting point.

steps: Flight to Apartadó (45 min) or bus (8h)
Shared van to Turbo (1.5h) or Necoclí (2.5h)
Direct flight Medellín (Olaya Herrera) → Capurganá, Las Brujas (50-55 min) with Clic Air
routes.note: 🆕 Since Nov. 2025: the most direct route. Book at clic-air.com or authorized agencies. Departs from Olaya Herrera — no trip to Rionegro needed.

steps: Flight to Acandí airport (1h) with Satena, SEARCA* or Moon Flights
routes.note: Most frequent route. *SEARCA operates the aircraft but tickets are sold by Nautilos or San Germán Express (not directly by SEARCA). Always confirm flight 24h ahead.

steps: Ground transport to Turbo (1.5-2h) or Necoclí (2-3h)
Bus to Montería (6h) or flight (45 min)
Head to the Necoclí or Turbo dock
el Caribe SAS speedboat: Necoclí → Capurganá → Sapzurro (1.5–2h)
Boats depart daily in the morning (~8:00 AM)
routes.note: Main operator: el Caribe SAS. Route: Necoclí → Capurganá → Sapzurro. Arrive at the dock 30 min early — seats sell out fast in high season.
Popular stopover for sailors heading to Cartagena

tips: Boats depart mainly in the morning (6-8 AM)
Bring cash: ATM in Capurganá but not always working
Pack light: maritime transport has luggage limits
Use biodegradable sunscreen to protect our reefs
La Miel day trip (Panama): just bring your ID — no passport needed
howToGetHere.mapDescription: Located in the Darién region of Colombia, right at the border with Panama. Sapzurro sits where the jungle meets the Caribbean Sea.
howToGetHere.needHelpDesc: We can help coordinate your entire journey from door to door. Get personalized travel advice and transportation arrangements.
howToGetHere.responseTime: We respond within 24 hours • We speak English & Spanish
